fields.description:
## Why
S2 (urge-brook-plume) named a `merge_change_409_guard` journald event in its plan's What section for the review-bot's merge-change path. S2 implemented the guard STRUCTURALLY — a merge revision (>= 2 parents) cannot use Gerrit's bare `/patch` endpoint (it returns HTTP 409 'Revision has too many parents'), so voter.review_and_vote branches on `is_merge` BEFORE any diff fetch and routes merges through the auto-merge-delta path (`_assemble_merge_diff`) instead of `gc.get_patch` (src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:393 vs :426; unit test asserts get_patch_calls==0 on merges). BUT the named observability event was NEVER emitted (`grep merge_change_409_guard src/` = 0 hits) — so the guard fires silently. S2's completion verifier flagged this as a valid gap; it was deferred to this follow-up.

## What
- Emit a structured `merge_change_409_guard` journald event (via the voter's `_emit`, same shape as `merge_detection`/`merge_change_review`) at the point the code enters the is_merge branch and deliberately avoids the bare `/patch` (which would 409 on a merge). Carry change_id, revision_id, parent_count.
- It fires ONLY on merges (is_merge True) — distinct from `merge_detection` (which logs is_merge for EVERY change).
- Document it in infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md's merge-change ops signals list.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `merge_change_409_guard` structured event is emitted when the review-bot takes the merge path (is_merge, avoiding the bare /patch 409)
- [ ] The event does NOT fire on a non-merge change (guard is merge-specific)
- [ ] A unit test asserts the event fires on a merge and is absent on a non-merge (src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py path via tests/unit/test_review_bot.py)
- [ ] Documented in infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md merge-change ops signals
